task: I need to send a short note to a coworker about a schedule change, and I want to make sure it includes the key details without getting messy. Can you help me put it together so it’s easy to send?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, clean template you can send:

**Hey [Name], just a quick note — I’ve moved our [meeting/task] to [new time/date] because [brief reason]. I’ll send the updated invite shortly.**

If you want, I can also make it:
- more casual
- more professional
- tailored to your exact schedule change

user_simulator.text: Yeah, close enough. But I need it a little more specific — just a plain coworker note, not a template with brackets all over it. Make it sound professional but not stiff, and keep it short. Something I can basically copy and send.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a cleaner, more natural version you can send:

**Hey [Name], just a quick heads-up that I had to move our meeting to [new day/time]. I’ll send the updated invite shortly.**

If you want, I can also make it a little more casual or a little firmer.

user_simulator.text: Better, but still too generic. I need it to sound like an actual coworker note, not a fill-in-the-blank. Just give me one short version I can send, and make it clear the schedule changed without sounding overly formal.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short coworker-style version you can send:

**Hey, just a heads-up that the schedule changed, so I’m moving our meeting to later today. I’ll send the updated invite in a bit.**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little more specific
- more casual
- or more direct

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and clear enough.
